extra.transcript:
```mermaid --- title: "Mermaid: Representative Artifact" --- flowchart TD Start([Start]) --> Input[/Read source text/] Input --> Lexer[Lexer
tokenizes input] Lexer --> Parser[Parser
builds AST] Parser --> Renderer{Diagram type?} Renderer -->|flowchart| Flow[Flowchart renderer] Renderer -->|sequence| Seq[Sequence renderer] Renderer -->|class| Cls[Class renderer] Renderer -->|state| St[State renderer] Renderer -->|gantt| Gnt[Gantt renderer] Flow --> Layout[Layout engine] Seq --> Layout Cls --> Layout St --> Layout Gnt --> Layout Layout --> SVG[SVG output] SVG --> Display([Render in browser]) classDef start end fill:#1f6feb,stroke:#0d47a1,color:#fff; classDef proc fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0,color:#000; classDef decide fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,color:#000; classDef term fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32,color:#000; class Start,Display term; class Input,Lexer,Parser,Flow,Seq,Cls,St,Gnt,Layout,SVG proc; class Renderer decide; ``` This is a representative Mermaid artifact: a self-describing `flowchart TD` that depicts Mermaid's own rendering pipeline. It exercises core Mermaid features so its correctness can be verified by rendering it in any Mermaid-capable viewer: - **Frontmatter / title** — YAML `---` block with `title`. - **Direction** — `flowchart TD` (top-down). - **Node shapes** — rounded `([Start])`, stadium, parallelogram `[/Read source text/]`, rectangle `[Lexer]`, rhombus `{Diagram type?}`. - **Edge labels** — `-->|flowchart|`, `-->|sequence|`, etc. - **HTML in labels** — `
` for line breaks inside `[Lexer
tokenizes input]`. - **Class definitions and styling** — `classDef` with fill/stroke/color, plus `class ... proc;` assignments. - **Multiple branches converging** — several renderers feeding a shared `Layout` node. Render this block (e.g., in a Markdown file processed by Mermaid, the Mermaid Live Editor at https://mermaid.live, or `@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli` via `mmdc -i file.mmd -o out.svg`) to confirm it parses and displays as intended.

Done. Mermaid's representative artifact is produced.

## Result

The documented artifact kind for Mermaid is **SVG** — a self-contained vector image that renders inline with no viewer needed (`out/diagram.svg`).

**Source (DSL)** — `out/diagram.mmd`:
```mermaid
---
title: Mermaid representative artifact
---
flowchart TD
  A[Start] --> B{ok?}
  B -->|yes| C[Done]
  B -->|no| A
```

**Rendered artifact** — `out/diagram.svg` (13.5 KB, valid SVG, `viewBox` set, single `<svg>…</svg>` root, white background).

How it was made (per the validation/smoke spec in `mermaid.md`):
- Wrote the canonical flowchart DSL to `out/diagram.mmd`.
- Rendered with the official `mmdc` CLI: `npx -p @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli mmdc -i out/diagram.mmd -o out/diagram.svg -b white`.
- Passed a puppeteer config (`out/puppeteer.json`) pointing at the locally installed Google Chrome (`--no-sandbox`), avoiding the puppeteer Chromium download step.

Files in `out/`:
- `diagram.mmd` — diffable plain-text source (the real "source of truth")
- `diagram.svg` — the representative artifact (vector, self-contained)
- `puppeteer.json` — render config

The SVG is the deliverable; the `.mmd` is the editable, diffable source that regenerates it.

**Artifact produced:** `mermaid-representative-artifact.mmd`

This is Mermaid's representative artifact — a **`.mmd` definition file**. Mermaid's defining characteristic is that it renders Markdown-inspired text into diagrams; the source text *is* the artifact (the rendered SVG/PNG is a derivative output).

Why this represents Mermaid well — it exercises the signature features of the `flowchart` type in a single compact file:

| Feature shown | Where in the file |
|---|---|
| Front-matter config (`init` with theme + curve) | Top line |
| Directional flow (`TD`) | `flowchart TD` |
| Node shapes — stadium `([...])`, parallelogram `[/.../]`, rhombus `{...}`, cylinder `[(...)]` | `Start`, `Input`, `Validate`, `Store` |
| Labeled edges | `Validate -- Yes -->`, `-- No -->` |
| Subgraph grouping | `subgraph CacheLayer` |
| Class-based styling + classDef | `:::startStyle`, `classDef ...` |
| Link styling | `linkStyle default` |
| A decision loop (error → re-input) and a cache hit/miss branch | `Error --> Input`, `Cache -- Hit/Miss` |

The Mermaid CLI (`mmdc`) isn't installed in this environment, so the text source is the deliverable. To render it to SVG, run:

```bash
npm i -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli
mmdc -i mermaid-representative-artifact.mmd -o mermaid-representative-artifact.svg
```

Or paste the contents into any Mermaid Live Editor (https://mermaid.live) to see it rendered immediately.
